source: User-identified architectural flaw + community deployment research
summary: Clone-test-replace uses fresh Hetzner VPS instances, not Docker containers

community_evidence.production_deployments: Docker's own position: 'An LLM deciding its own security boundaries is not a security model'
community_evidence.practitioner_sentiment: MicroVM and full-VPS testing emerging as best practice for system-level agent changes

evaluation.cost_of_switching: ~EUR 0.05 per test cycle (4 hours on CX23 + snapshot)
evaluation.cost_of_staying: Docker cannot test system-level changes — packages, services, network, Docker itself

rationale: CTO has full system access. Macro evolution can change anything including OS packages, system services, network config, and Docker itself. A Docker container shares the host kernel and cannot faithfully test these changes. Only a full VPS provides a true test environment. Hetzner API enables programmatic provisioning and destruction. Cost per test cycle is ~EUR 0.05.

test_results.notes: Hetzner API researched. hcloud CLI available. Community Hetzner MCP server exists with 60 tools.

rollback.instructions: Revert to Docker-based testing if VPS approach proves impractical
